sections.title: A: Multiple Choice (Criticism)
sections.instructions: Choose the correct answer (A, B, C, or D).

items.sentence: 1 A be B have C take D do
2 A talented B invested C mixed D workable
3 A alert B clever C intelligent D aware
4 A overflowing B full C filled D packed
5 A mind B brain C thought D idea
6 A cease B remove C avoid D prevent
7 A outcome B result C effect D consequence
8 A lacking B short C missing D absent
9 A suggests B advises C proposes D explains
10 A think B consider C look round D take
11 A career B business C job D work
12 A mess B rubbish C trash D garbage
13 A put off B bank on C keep on D drop in on
14 A require B need C depend D trust
15 A turn out B come into C deal with D sail through

sections.title: B: Multiple Choice (Vocabulary)
sections.instructions: Choose the correct answer (A, B, C, or D).

items.sentence: 16 Ed ______ at the clock and realised he was late... A glimpsed B observed C glanced D watched
17 What's the entrance ______ for the outdoor music festival? A ticket B fare C price D fee
18 Don't ______ the potatoes in oil; that's so unhealthy! A fry B boil C bake D grill
19 I can't find the ______ anywhere. A recipe B receipt C receiver D reception
20 I'll pick up a ______ on the way home... A takeaway B fast food C delivery D microwave
21 Certificates provide proof of your ______. A qualities B diplomas C qualifications D ambitions
22 ...ours is about the most ______ lifestyle you can have! A urban B suburban C rural D municipal
23 Will was made ______ three months ago... A sacked B fired C retired D redundant
24 The audience showed their ______ by giving a standing ovation. A entertainment B review C appreciation D audition
25 I'd like to speak to the person in ______ please. A charge B responsibility C duty D obligation

sections.title: C: Open Cloze (Lack of faith in politicians)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: 26 A recent survey has shown an increase ______ distrust for politicians.
27 They've always been regarded ______ untrustworthy...
28 A majority of the people who ______ questioned...
29 ...associate politicians ______ an inability...
30 ...to tell ______ truth.
31 They believe they're responsible ______ most of the country's problems...
32 ...have had a negative effect ______ the country's image...
33 ...refuse to face ______ to the fact...
34 ...never apologise for ______ made mistakes.
35 The survey, ______ was carried out over three months...
36 ...give the impression ______ being arrogant...
37 ...only ______ note of what the electorate believe...
38 In ______ of these disturbing findings...
39 ...people do not believe that ______ is no point in voting...
40 ______ least politicians can take some comfort...

sections.title: D: Multiple Choice (Grammar)
sections.instructions: Choose the correct answer (A, B, C, or D).

items.sentence: 41 All the flowers for the wedding tomorrow ______. A will be delivering B will have delivered by C are going to deliver D are going to be delivered
42 'Have we still got lots of time?' 'No, ______ get a move on...' A you'd rather B you'd better C you'd prefer to D you'd be better off
43 Let's order a pizza, ______ we? A should B will C shall D could
44 I wonder ______ mind watching this bag for me... A would you B you would C if would you D whether you would
45 The prime minister denied ______ anything wrong. A to do B having done C to have done D to be doing
46 The woman ______ purse I found has offered a reward. A whose B who C who's D to whom
47 It's high time they ______. A are leaving B were leaving C have to leave D had been leaving
48 I wish you ______ so rude to Rosalind last night. A weren't B wouldn't be C hadn't been D haven't been
49 Despite ______ president, she felt totally powerless. A being B she was C of having been D to be
50 We ______ today and I got into trouble because I hadn't done it. A had checked our homework B had our homework checked C were checked our homework D have checking our homework

sections.title: E: Matching (Phrasal Verbs)
sections.instructions: Match to make sentences. There is one extra letter you will not use.

items.sentence: 51 When we can afford it, we're going to do
52 Jason's thinking of dropping
53 When the factory closed
54 They should do away
55 Let me take you out to dinner to make
56 I never wanted a mortgage but I'm coming
57 Cynthia's decided to set

sections.title: F: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
58 I don't think George has the ability to climb right to the top of that mountain! (capable)
I don't think George ______ right to the top of that mountain!

59 Joanne, do you know anything about Chaos Theory? (familiar)
Joanne, ______ Chaos Theory?

60 You shouldn't have bought presents for all of us. (expense)
You shouldn't have ______ buying presents for all of us.

61 Charles often phones up TV stations to complain about programmes. (tendency)
Charles ______ up TV stations to complain about programmes.

62 Alice seemed tired to me. (impression)
Alice ______ she was tired.

63 Taking out a private pension scheme will benefit you. (interest)
It is ______ out a private pension scheme.

64 I understand what Gary was saying but I don't agree with him. (point)
I can ______ but I don't agree with him.

65 They won't allow passengers to get off the train at the next station. (prevent)
They're going ______ off the train at the next station.

66 I want you to try really hard to pass this exam! (effort)
I want you to ______ to pass this exam!

sections.title: G: Error Correction (Extra word)
sections.instructions: If a line is correct, put a tick (✓). If there is an extra word, write it.

items.sentence: 67 It is often been said that the secret of good comedy is timing.
68 As a comedian myself, I know that's not true.
69 I was told that a joke the other day about a man whose his dog has no nose.
70 When asked how it smells, the man replies 'Terrible!'. The problem here is that, despite of the joke having a fairly clever punchline...
71 ...no one finds it a funny.
72 However good your timing is, you can't make out people laugh at that joke.
73 I get my friends to tell to me jokes all the time.
74 If I've never heard them before, and if they make me laugh it out loud...
75 I am like jokes that rely on word play...
76 ...even though the joke was about the noseless dog is rubbish!
77 'My mum's from Cuba and my dad's from Iceland so I guess that makes me up an ice cube.'
78 Now that's a classic joke however you tell it.
79 Comedy is made not just to do with timing!
80 (blank line - no text provided in PDF for line 80 in this section)
81 (blank line - no text provided in PDF for line 81 in this section)

sections.title: H: Word Formation (Memories and reality)
sections.instructions: Use the word in capitals to form a word that fits in the gap.

items.sentence: 82 There is a well-known ______, 'School days are the best days of your life.' (SAY)
83 No ______ at school ever believes it though... (STUDY)
84 ...when you're worried about your ______ situation... (FINANCE)
85 ...and are beginning to hate the ______ of working in an office... (BORE)
86 ...you look back fondly on the ______ times you had at school. (EXCITE)
87 ...like having to pay ______ during a tedious chemistry lesson... (ATTEND)
88 ...being punished for bad ______ when you didn't do anything wrong... (BEHAVE)
89 ...or feeling ______ in an exam because you don't know any of the answers... (HELP)
90 ...and, in fact, had completely ______ the questions too! (UNDERSTAND)
91 But it's quite ______ to complain about things and then have great memories afterwards. (ACCEPT)
